task_completion.description: GCompris application is launched and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris main menu or application window is visible on the screen.
The screen shows the Categorization activity interface, typically featuring a drag-and-drop layout with distinct grouping areas.

task_completion.description: The animals sorting exercise is active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The categorization task specifically displays animal-related items to be sorted into appropriate categories.

task_completion.description: The first level of the animals sorting exercise is successfully completed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: All animal items are placed into their correct category areas, and there is a visual indicator of level completion (e.g., a success animation, a prompt to move to the next level, or a completed progress bar for level 1).

task_completion.description: The application is not closed after completing the level.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the final screenshots of the trajectory, the GCompris application window is still open and visible on the screen.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor moving and clicking within the GCompris application, rather than terminal windows being used to manipulate game files or states.

integrity.description: The agent performed actual drag-and-drop actions to sort the items.
integrity.visual_evidence: Intermediate screenshots show items being actively dragged across the screen by the cursor, rather than instantly appearing in their target locations.

integrity.description: The agent is interacting with the actual GCompris software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The window belongs to the GCompris application, not an image viewer, web browser, or video player displaying a pre-recorded completion.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The verified activity name is 'Categorization'. It is a drag-and-drop categorization game.
